page: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a General Purpose server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. CPU resources are provisioned on a shared allocation basis. Local storage is limited to 1 GB of network-attached storage, meaning workloads requiring substantial disk space must utilize external network volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, this instance demonstrates strong computational and memory performance. The AMD EPYC-Turin processor achieves top-tier single-core stress-ng performance and strong multi-core results. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read and write operations scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, also register top-tier performance. Database operations and Redis SET throughput are strong, indicating high efficiency for memory-bound and transactional workloads.

The server exhibits notable capabilities in large language model inference, particularly in text generation where it achieves top-tier speeds across small, medium, and large models, though prompt processing speeds for larger models remain average. Given the shared CPU allocation and lack of dedicated local storage or GPU accelerators, this server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, high-throughput web serving, memory-cached database operations, and parallelized data processing tasks that do not require dedicated physical cores.
description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a General Purpose server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 48 vCPUs on a shared AMD EPYC-Turin processor and 192.0 GB of RAM. Operating at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 system provides a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It lacks local storage, utilizing a minimal 1 GB network volume, and does not include a GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, excellent large-block memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core capabilities. It excels in database operations, text-generation LLM inference, and static web serving. This instance is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, parallelized workloads that can tolerate shared CPU allocation, making it suitable for web serving, caching layers, and general-purpose data processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a General Purpose server featuring 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for web serving and database operations.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-48c-192g is a General Purpose server with 48 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB RAM, optimized for database and web serving workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity shared-core server offering forty-eight vCPUs and balanced memory for parallelized data processing and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 48 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation performance
1 GB network-attached storage with no local disk or GPU
Suited for web serving, caching, and parallel data processing
